title: Spicy Gooey Peruvian Chicken Stir fry
description: A spicy and romantic Peruvian dish featuring rosemary and a gooey sauce, perfect for a low-fat friendly dinner. This stir-fry combines the flavors of Peru with a hint of rosemary and a kick of heat.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped rosemary and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the chopped aji amarillo peppers and minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes, until the peppers start to soften.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, honey, and rice vinegar.
Pour the sauce into the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Return the chicken to the skillet and toss to coat with the sauce.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the chicken is coated.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with additional rosemary if desired.
